root.name: Add a slide after slide 2 with a table comparing characteristics of all three worm types
root.description: Evaluates whether the agent has correctly added a slide after slide 2 that contains a table comparing characteristics of all three worm types, and that the result is accurate and precise without introducing extraneous or erroneous content.

children.name: Exactly one slide added
children.description: Verifies that one and only one slide was inserted.

children.name: Table Presence and Structure
children.description: Checks that the inserted slide contains a table and that the table is formatted to compare all three worm types in a meaningful way.

children.name: Table exists on inserted slide
children.description: Verifies that the new slide contains a table element.

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    from pptx import Presentation
    pres = Presentation(modified_ppt_path)
    # Slides are 0-indexed in python-pptx
    slide_idx = 2  # Slide after slide 2 is index 2
    if slide_idx >= len(pres.slides):
        return "Inserted slide not found in the modified presentation.", 0.0
    slide = pres.slides[slide_idx]
    table_found = any(shape.has_table for shape in slide.shapes)
    if not table_found:
        return "No table found on inserted slide.", 0.0
    return "Table found on the inserted slide.", 1.0

children.name: Table structure allows comparison of all three worm types
children.description: Verifies that the table has at least three columns or three rows for worm types and is likely structured for comparison.

children.name: Table Content Quality
children.description: Checks that the table is indeed comparing characteristics of all three worm types, and not some other content. Also checks if the comparison is reasonably accurate.

children.name: Comparison of all three worm types present
children.description: Checks that all three worm types are listed as headers or row/column titles in the table.

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    from pptx import Presentation
    expected_types = ["roundworm", "flatworm", "segmented worm"]
    pres = Presentation(modified_ppt_path)
    slide_idx = 2
    slide = pres.slides[slide_idx]
    for shape in slide.shapes:
        if shape.has_table:
            tbl = shape.table
            text_cells = set(cell.text.strip().lower() for row in tbl.rows for cell in row.cells)
            # Simple matching by substring
            found = [any(t in cell for cell in text_cells) for t in expected_types]
            if all(found):
                return "All three worm types found as table headers or labels.", 1.0
            else:
                missing = [t for i, t in enumerate(expected_types) if not found[i]]
                return f"Missing worm types in table: {', '.join(missing)}.", 0.0
    return "No table found to check worm types.", 0.0

children.name: Table compares characteristics (not just lists names)
children.description: Checks that the table contains more than just worm type names, i.e., there are other rows/columns with different characteristics being compared.

children.name: Table content is relevant and not extraneous
children.description: Checks that the table does not contain unrelated or extraneous data (e.g., animals unrelated to worms).

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    from pptx import Presentation
    pres = Presentation(modified_ppt_path)
    slide_idx = 2
    slide = pres.slides[slide_idx]
    unrelated_words = ["mammal", "bird", "fish", "reptile", "amphibian", "insect", "spider", "dog", "cat", "elephant", "snake"]
    for shape in slide.shapes:
        if shape.has_table:
            tbl = shape.table
            all_text = " ".join(cell.text.lower() for row in tbl.rows for cell in row.cells)
            if any(w in all_text for w in unrelated_words):
                return "Table contains unrelated or extraneous content.", 0.0
            return "No unrelated or extraneous content in table.", 1.0
    return "No table found to check extraneous content.", 1.0  # N/A, so don't penalize

children.name: No Unrelated Changes
children.description: Ensures that the agent did not introduce any unrelated or extraneous changes to the presentation, such as modifying or deleting other slides or content.

children.name: No unrelated slides added or removed
children.description: Checks that no slides other than the required new slide were added or removed.

children.name: No unrelated modifications to other slides
children.description: Checks that no slides other than the new slide were modified (apart from possible slide number shifts).

metadata.task: Add a slide after slide 2 with a table comparing characteristics of all three worm types
